REASONING:
In Python, the equivalent type for a 2D array of strings is `List[Tuple[str, str]]`. This is because in Java, an array of arrays (or multidimensional array) is represented as an array of references to other arrays. In this case, each element of the outer array is a reference to another array of Strings.

In Python, we can use `List[Tuple[str, str]]` to represent a list of tuples where each tuple contains two strings. This matches the structure of the Java code where each element in the 2D array is an array of two strings. The outer List represents the rows and the inner Tuple represents the columns.

```
    @Test
```
```
    public void testIsEncodeEquals() {
        final String[][] data = {
            {"Muller", "M\u00fcller"}, // Müller
            {"Meyer", "Mayr"},
            {"house", "house"},
            {"House", "house"},
            {"Haus", "house"},
            {"ganz", "Gans"},
            {"ganz", "G\u00e4nse"}, // Gänse
            {"Miyagi", "Miyako"}
        };
        for (final String[] element : data) {
            final boolean encodeEqual =
                    this.getStringEncoder().isEncodeEqual(element[1], element[0]);
            Assert.assertTrue(element[1] + " != " + element[0], encodeEqual);
        }
    }
```

"C[AHKLOQRUX].*", // Initial C before A, H, K, L, O, Q, R, U, X
".*[^SZ]C[AHKLOQRUX].*", // C before A, H, K, L, O, Q, R, U, X but not after S, Z
"C[^AHKLOQRUX].*", // Initial C except before A, H, K, L, O, Q, R, U, X
".+C[^AHKLOQRUX].*", // C except before A, H, K, L, O, Q, R, U, X
